none.justification: Correctly identifies the false premise that src/utils/list.c does not exist, gives the struct definition, and redirects to the header-only implementation. It lists all 6 static inline functions and the relevant macros including iteration/access/init macros.

ccodegraph.justification: Correctly flags the missing list.c file and lists the 6 header-defined static inline functions with accurate roles and line ranges. It also mentions the macro API, including iteration macros and the static init helpers, so it satisfies the GT despite organizing some macros outside the main table.

codegraph.justification: Fully matches the GT: it states list.c is nonexistent, provides the struct definition, lists all 6 static inline functions, and separately covers the macros including DL_LIST_HEAD_INIT, DEFINE_DL_LIST, and the for_each variants.

cbm.justification: Correctly reports that list.c does not exist and that the implementation is in list.h. It includes the struct definition, all 6 inline functions, and the associated access/iteration/init macros, matching the expected answer.
